mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing, which lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and mechanically raises present valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-trend periods do not show statistically higher mean returns (t-test p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month span, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades and risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances show positive mean 20d forward returns in more than 55% of cases, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline returns (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth (RSAFS) combined with a stable or falling federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) jointly signal robust consumer demand without tightening monetary headwinds, reinforcing risk appetite and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon. Neither series alone captures this synergy since strong demand under tightening policy has historically been offset by valuation compression.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.3% AND FEDFUNDS flat or declining over trailing 2 months
regime_hint: monetary easing or pause combined with demand expansion
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods fail to outperform periods where only RSAFS is strong (without the FEDFUNDS condition) by a statistically significant margin, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over univariate RSAFS model.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a deceleration in INDPRO growth signals approaching capacity constraints or demand exhaustion, causing markets to reprice near-term risk downward and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon as traders react quickly to the print.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration shows no significant negative association with 5-day forward returns during identified late-cycle windows (Rank IC < 0.02), or effect reverses sign in more than one distinct late-cycle episode.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in PAYEMS reflects expanding labor income and consumer spending capacity, which supports corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon as the effect compounds through the earnings cycle.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS 3-month growth trend and forward 60d returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or positive PAYEMS growth periods underperform negative growth periods on average returns.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST exceeding 10% over 3 months signals housing market contraction, a leading indicator of broader consumer demand weakness and credit tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the signal propagates to cyclical equity sectors.
failure_condition: Triggered HOUST decline episodes show non-negative mean forward 20d returns, or fewer than 60% of triggered instances exhibit negative returns across the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains low, markets anticipate an imminent policy tightening surprise, generating short-term volatility and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as investors reposition ahead of rate decisions. The interaction is necessary because rising inflation with already-high rates does not carry the same surprise value.
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes do not show significantly more negative 5-day forward returns than episodes with high inflation alone, or Rank IC for the interaction term is indistinguishable from zero (|IC|<0.02).

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, decelerating GDPC1 growth signals the recovery is stalling before reaching trend potential, prompting markets to discount future growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon, whereas in mid-cycle regimes the same deceleration is a benign soft-landing signal with no directional effect.
failure_condition: GDPC1 deceleration during identified post-shock recovery windows shows no significant negative relationship with 60d forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or the sign of the effect is inconsistent across multiple recovery episodes.
